parts.title: Transcript
parts.annotated_transcript_html:
Speaker 1: This recording is copyright. You will hear a woman phoning a bank to find out about opening a bank account. You now have some time to look at the questions on your screen. Now, answer the questions on your screen.

Speaker 2: That's great. So, if you'd like to open an account, I'll just need some details.

Speaker 1: Sure. Can I start with your name, please?

Speaker 2: Lisa Ashbury.

Speaker 1: Can I just check the spelling of your surname? Is that A-S-H...?

Speaker 2: That's right. And then B-E-double-R-Y.

Speaker 1: OK, I've got that. Thank you very much. And your address, please?

Speaker 2: Of course. It's No. 20, Pricely Lane, and that's in Longstone.

Speaker 1: Right, got that. Thank you. And can I take a postcode, please?

Speaker 2: It's W23K594.

Speaker 1: Thank you. I'll just be a second entering that.

Speaker 2: Sure, no problem.

Speaker 1: And I need a phone number.

Speaker 2: OK. It's 087-958-0662.

Speaker 1: OK. So, is this to be an individual account, then? Is it just in your own name?

Speaker 2: No, I want to open an account for me and my brother.

Speaker 1: Ah, so it's a joint one rather than an individual account.

Speaker 2: Oh, yes. Sorry. That's right.

Speaker 1: OK, that shouldn't be a problem. I will need a few more details, though.

Speaker 2: That's fine.

Speaker 1: So, could you tell me what the account will be used for, please?

Speaker 2: Well, it's not for my salary and bills. I have a separate account for that. But I'm about to start receiving some rent. It's for a holiday apartment, you see. And so this is for depositing that.

Speaker 1: OK, thank you. Right, I now need to read you our terms and conditions.
